lines.text: "Where the glowing paste came into contact with his skin, a strange light traced his veins." That's how the chapter ends. With contamination.
Adrian just shook hands with whatever Captain Gupta has become, and now it's inside him.

Right, and I feel it somehow connects to the opening line. "I was beginning to think the green outside my tent had an appetite for me." It's a frame that tells us whoever's narrating has survived something before this, something organic and hungry.
Okay. So now we know what happened to the the Bris. It's... well, let's start with that med-bay scene. The man watching his own heart being examined. Imagine.

The body horror is one thing, but what unsettles me more is the joy. They are all so... blissful. They're exploring themselves with childlike wonder. Using butchery and orgy as science. Those children in the leisure hall, being anointed as their "individuality dissolved". Nyland isn't subtle about the violation there.
They keep talking about unity, "the all." When the whole observation deck speaks in unison and says "We were the Beholder. Now, the paint. Just a drop. A single thought, a single stroke.".

That line is doing a lot of work. That’s a demotion from observer to medium. From personhood to instrument. A brush doesn’t decide the painting. Something else does. So who is the painter? 

Philosophically, it flirts with determinism and the terrifying idea that individuality is just a temporary illusion—that consciousness is merely a droplet briefly believing itself separate from the ocean. A single thought, a single stroke suggests agency has been absorbed into some larger intentionality.

And there’s something almost religious in it, too. Mystical traditions talk about union with the divine as transcendence. But here? It feels invasive. Reversed.
What do you make of Adrian Farley? He's tuning in."
I love him. He's the only one treating this as first contact. When Gupta talks about transcendence, Adrian actually engages philosophically."
God, and now the light is in his veins. Did Adrian just decide to join them?
I don't think it's that simple. But yeah. I'm curious to see how that unfolds.
And maybe our listeners are too. Question for you. Is this still a rescue mission?
